item.title: Meet ‘Your Rich BFF,’ the former JPMorgan trader and TikTok star who wants you to talk about money on the first date
item.description: Vivian Tu, who refers to herself as “your favorite Wall Street girly,” also hosts the “Networth and Chill" podcast and published a book, “Well Endowed...

item.title: CFO whose business fell 10% from tariffs cheers Supreme Court ruling: ‘No single person should have unilateral authority over tariffs’
item.description: Matt Totsch, the CFO of manufacturer Trim-Tex, said businesses like his have been dealing with crippling uncertainty caused by Trump’s chaotic tariff ...

item.title: New York nurses union wins 12% raise, AI safeguards in a tentative deal to end monthlong strike
item.description: Over the past month, nurses had picketed during bitterly cold temperatures in what their union said was the largest and longest walkout in the city’s ...

item.title: Wall Street shrugs at widely expected Supreme Court tariff ruling
item.description: The S&P 500 was 0.1% higher in midday trading.

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein exploited colleges’ ‘tremendous drive to acquire money’
item.description: Schools try to make sure there is no financial conflict of interest for the donor, researcher or institution. Emphasis on the "try."
